root_text.sn50.1-12:1.5: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, gaṅgā nadī pācīnaninnā pācīnapoṇā pācīnapabbhārā;
root_text.sn50.1-12:1.6: evameva kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañca balāni bhāvento pañca balāni bahulīkaronto nibbānaninno hoti nibbānapoṇo nibbānapabbhāro.
root_text.sn50.1-12:1.7: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañca balāni bhāvento pañca balāni bahulīkaronto nibbānaninno hoti nibbānapoṇo nibbānapabbhāro?
root_text.sn50.1-12:1.8: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddhābalaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ,
root_text.sn50.1-12:1.12: paññābalaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vossaggapariṇāmiṁ.
root_text.sn50.1-12:1.13: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañca balāni bhāvento pañca balāni bahulīkaronto nibbānaninno hoti nibbānapoṇo nibbānapabbhāro”ti.

translation_text.sn50.1-12:1.3: The powers of faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom.
translation_text.sn50.1-12:1.5: The Ganges river slants, slopes, and inclines to the east.
translation_text.sn50.1-12:1.6: In the same way, a mendicant who develops and cultivates the five powers slants, slopes, and inclines to extinguishment.
translation_text.sn50.1-12:1.7: And how does a mendicant who develops the five powers slant, slope, and incline to extinguishment?
translation_text.sn50.1-12:1.8: It’s when a mendicant develops the powers of faith,
translation_text.sn50.1-12:1.12: and wisdom, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.
translation_text.sn50.1-12:1.13: That’s how a mendicant who develops and cultivates the five powers slants, slopes, and inclines to extinguishment.”
translation_text.sn50.1-12:2.1: Six on slanting to the east,
translation_text.sn50.1-12:2.2: and six on slanting to the ocean;
translation_text.sn50.1-12:2.4: and that’s how this chapter is recited.
